title: Binary Search
prompt:
Given a sorted array of integers nums and an integer target, return the index of target if it is in nums. Otherwise, return -1.

You must write an algorithm with O(log n) runtime complexity.

The array is sorted in ascending order. No duplicate elements are guaranteed.
constraints: nums is sorted in ascending order

starterCode.typescript:
export function solve(nums: number[], target: number): number {
  
}

notesForAI.coreIdea: Classic binary search: maintain left and right pointers. Compute mid = (left + right) // 2. If nums[mid] == target, return mid. If nums[mid] < target, search right half. Otherwise search left half. Handle empty array.
commonBugs: Infinite loop from mid calculation: use (left + right) // 2 or left + (right - left) // 2 to avoid overflow
Off-by-one in boundary updates (left = mid + 1 vs left = mid)
